sections.title: рҹ§’ The Story
sections.rawMarkdown:
```
Without CI/CD:
вҶ’ developer merges code
вҶ’ someone builds manually on laptop
вҶ’ someone SSHs into server
вҶ’ restarts app and hopes it works

Problems:
вҶ’ no repeatability
вҶ’ no audit trail
вҶ’ deploys depend on one hero engineer
вҶ’ rollback is stressful and slow

CI/CD turns releases into a defined system:
commit вҶ’ build вҶ’ test вҶ’ deploy вҶ’ verify вҶ’ rollback if needed
```

---

blocks.content:
Without CI/CD:
вҶ’ developer merges code
вҶ’ someone builds manually on laptop
вҶ’ someone SSHs into server
вҶ’ restarts app and hopes it works

Problems:

вҶ’ deploys depend on one hero engineer
вҶ’ rollback is stressful and slow

CI/CD turns releases into a defined system:
commit вҶ’ build вҶ’ test вҶ’ deploy вҶ’ verify вҶ’ rollback if needed

sections.title: рҹ”Ғ Core Services
sections.rawMarkdown:
### CI/CD Big Picture Diagram
```
Developer push / merge
        в”Ӯ
        в–ј
   CodePipeline
        в”Ӯ
   в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
   в–ј    в–ј               в–ј
Source Build         Deploy
      CodeBuild   CodeDeploy / CloudFormation
        в”Ӯ               в”Ӯ
        в–ј               в–ј
   tests + artifact   traffic shift
        в”Ӯ               в”Ӯ
        в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв–ә verify with alarms в—„в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
                        в”Ӯ
                 healthy? yes/no
                    в”Ӯ         в”Ӯ
                    в–ј         в–ј
                 success   rollback

MENTAL MODEL:
CI/CD is a controlled path for change, not just a script that runs commands.
```

### CodePipeline
```
CodePipeline = orchestration layer
вҶ’ defines stages: Source вҶ’ Build вҶ’ Test вҶ’ Deploy
вҶ’ integrates with GitHub, CodeBuild, CodeDeploy, CloudFormation
вҶ’ visual pipeline state and history
```

### CodeBuild
```
CodeBuild = managed build runner
вҶ’ compiles code, runs tests, builds Docker images
вҶ’ ephemeral environment per build
вҶ’ buildspec.yml defines commands

Example buildspec phases:
install  вҶ’ dependencies
pre_build вҶ’ auth/login/setup
build    вҶ’ tests + package/build
post_build вҶ’ push image / create artifacts
```

### CodeDeploy
```
CodeDeploy = deployment engine
вҶ’ deploy to EC2, ECS, Lambda
вҶ’ rolling, blue/green, canary styles depending on target
вҶ’ lifecycle hooks for validation before/after traffic shift
```

---

sections.title: рҹҡҰ Deployment Strategies
sections.rawMarkdown:
```
ROLLING:
вҶ’ replace instances/tasks gradually
вҶ’ lower extra cost
вҶ’ slower rollback than true blue/green

BLUE/GREEN:
вҶ’ old version and new version run side by side
вҶ’ shift traffic when green proves healthy
вҶ’ fastest rollback: move traffic back to blue

CANARY:
вҶ’ small percentage first
вҶ’ good for catching production-only issues early
```

---

blocks.content:
ROLLING:
вҶ’ replace instances/tasks gradually
вҶ’ lower extra cost
вҶ’ slower rollback than true blue/green

BLUE/GREEN:
вҶ’ old version and new version run side by side
вҶ’ shift traffic when green proves healthy
вҶ’ fastest rollback: move traffic back to blue

CANARY:
вҶ’ small percentage first
вҶ’ good for catching production-only issues early

sections.title: рҹ’Ў CI/CD Best Practices
sections.rawMarkdown:
```
вң… small frequent releases
вң… automated tests before deploy
вң… immutable artifacts (same artifact moves across environments)
вң… separate build from deploy
вң… health checks and smoke tests after deploy
вң… automated rollback criteria on alarms
вң… infrastructure changes go through pipeline too, not only app code
```

---

blocks.content:
вң… small frequent releases
вң… automated tests before deploy
вң… immutable artifacts (same artifact moves across environments)
вң… separate build from deploy
вң… health checks and smoke tests after deploy
вң… automated rollback criteria on alarms
вң… infrastructure changes go through pipeline too, not only app code

sections.title: рҹҺҜ Interview Q&A вҖ” CI/CD
sections.rawMarkdown:
### CI/CD Real-World Failure Flow

```
Code merges
   в”Ӯ
   в–ј
Pipeline builds artifact
   в”Ӯ
   в–ј
Deploy starts
   в”Ӯ
   в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
   в–ј    в–ј                              в–ј
bad artifact  env/config mismatch      hidden prod-only issue
   в”Ӯ            в”Ӯ                       в”Ӯ
   в–ј            в–ј                       в–ј
tests passed   deployment unstable   alarms fire after traffic shift
   в”Ӯ            в”Ӯ                       в”Ӯ
   в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
                              в–ј
                        rollback decision
                              в”Ӯ
                              в–ј
                  restore known-good version

SENIOR MENTAL MODEL:
The pipeline is not done when deployment finishes.
It is done when health verification passes and rollback confidence is preserved.
```

**Q: What makes a deployment pipeline production-grade?**
```
"Not just automation. A production-grade pipeline has safety.

I look for:
вҶ’ immutable artifacts
вҶ’ automated tests
вҶ’ environment promotion path
вҶ’ approvals where needed
вҶ’ health verification after deployment
вҶ’ rollback mechanism tied to alarms

A script that copies files to prod is automation.
A pipeline that reduces change risk is CI/CD."
```

**Q: How do you reduce deployment risk for critical services?**
```
"Use progressive delivery.

My preferred sequence:
вҶ’ deploy to staging with realistic tests
вҶ’ blue/green or canary in production
вҶ’ watch latency, errors, saturation, business KPIs
вҶ’ auto-rollback if thresholds breach

Senior delivery is about limiting blast radius, not just making deployment faster."
```

**Q: Why separate CodeBuild from CodeDeploy instead of doing everything in one script?**
```
"Because build and deploy solve different problems.

Build should create a tested immutable artifact.
Deploy should promote that exact artifact into environments.

If you rebuild per environment, you lose traceability and increase drift.
Separation gives you reproducibility, auditing, and safer rollback."
```

---

SENIOR MENTAL MODEL:

**Q: What makes a deployment pipeline production-grade?**

"Not just automation. A production-grade pipeline has safety.

I look for:
вҶ’ immutable artifacts
вҶ’ automated tests
вҶ’ environment promotion path
вҶ’ approvals where needed
вҶ’ health verification after deployment
вҶ’ rollback mechanism tied to alarms

A script that copies files to prod is automation.
A pipeline that reduces change risk is CI/CD."
**Q: How do you reduce deployment risk for critical services?**

"Use progressive delivery.

My preferred sequence:
вҶ’ deploy to staging with realistic tests
вҶ’ blue/green or canary in production
вҶ’ watch latency, errors, saturation, business KPIs
вҶ’ auto-rollback if thresholds breach

Senior delivery is about limiting blast radius, not just making deployment faster."
**Q: Why separate CodeBuild from CodeDeploy instead of doing everything in one script?**

"Because build and deploy solve different problems.

Build should create a tested immutable artifact.
Deploy should promote that exact artifact into environments.

If you rebuild per environment, you lose traceability and increase drift.
Separation gives you reproducibility, auditing, and safer rollback."

sections.title: рҹ”— Connects To
sections.rawMarkdown:
```
CodePipeline вҶҗ GitHub/CodeCommit: source changes
CodePipeline вҶ’ CodeBuild: build and test
CodePipeline вҶ’ CloudFormation/CodeDeploy/ECS/Lambda: deploy targets
CodeBuild вҶ’ ECR/S3: store built artifacts and images
CodeDeploy вҶ’ ALB/Lambda/ECS: traffic shifting and rollout control
CI/CD вҶ’ CloudWatch alarms: deployment verification and rollback triggers

NEXT: Well-Architected + Architecture Patterns
How to judge whether the whole system is actually well designed.
```

---
